questions.question: Define statistics and explain its importance.
questions.answer: Statistics is the branch of mathematics dealing with the collection, organisation, presentation, analysis and interpretation of data. It is important for decision making, planning and understanding numerical information.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by data with examples.
questions.answer: Data are numerical facts collected for a specific purpose. Examples include marks of students, heights of people and daily temperatures.

questions.question: Explain frequency and frequency distribution.
questions.answer: Frequency is the number of times a value occurs. A frequency distribution arranges data values along with their frequencies in a table.

questions.question: Explain grouped and ungrouped data.
questions.answer: Ungrouped data is raw data not arranged in classes, while grouped data is arranged into class intervals for better understanding.

questions.question: Define class interval and class size.
questions.answer: Class interval is the range in which data is grouped. Class size is the difference between upper and lower class limits.

questions.question: Explain mean as a measure of central tendency.
questions.answer: Mean is the average of all observations and is obtained by dividing the sum of values by the number of observations.

questions.question: Explain median as a measure of central tendency.
questions.answer: Median is the middle value of ordered data and divides the data into two equal parts.

questions.question: Explain mode as a measure of central tendency.
questions.answer: Mode is the value that occurs most frequently in a data set.

questions.question: Explain cumulative frequency.
questions.answer: Cumulative frequency is the running total of frequencies and helps in finding median graphically.

questions.question: Explain bar graph.
questions.answer: A bar graph represents discrete data using rectangular bars of equal width.

questions.question: Explain histogram.
questions.answer: A histogram represents continuous data using adjacent rectangles.

questions.question: Explain ogive.
questions.answer: Ogive is a cumulative frequency curve used to locate median graphically.

questions.question: Explain population and sample.
questions.answer: Population is the entire group under study, while a sample is a part selected from the population.

questions.question: Explain discrete and continuous data.
questions.answer: Discrete data consists of countable values, while continuous data can take any value in a range.

questions.question: Explain raw data and tabulation.
questions.answer: Raw data is original unorganised data. Tabulation is arranging data systematically in tables.

questions.question: Explain tally marks.
questions.answer: Tally marks are used to count frequencies easily and accurately.

questions.question: Explain frequency polygon.
questions.answer: A frequency polygon is formed by joining midpoints of class intervals.

questions.question: Explain class mark.
questions.answer: Class mark is the mid-point of a class interval and represents the class.

questions.question: Explain frequency table.
questions.answer: A frequency table shows data values along with their frequencies.

questions.question: Explain statistical enquiry.
questions.answer: Statistical enquiry is the systematic collection of data for analysis.

questions.question: Explain observation in statistics.
questions.answer: Each individual value in a data set is called an observation.

questions.question: Explain range.
questions.answer: Range is the difference between the highest and lowest values of data.

questions.question: Explain frequency curve.
questions.answer: Frequency curve is a smooth curve representing frequency distribution.

questions.question: Explain data presentation.
questions.answer: Data presentation makes data understandable using tables and graphs.

questions.question: Explain importance of statistics.
questions.answer: Statistics helps in planning, decision making and analysis in various fields.
